lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Hello there...thanks for helping out Trufitus commune with the gods Bwana. We've been able to return to the village thanks to your help.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: I see that you've been hacking away at the jungle...good, now if you have some thatching spars you can go and fix the fence. It's just to the south near the jungle, again, you'll need a machete for this.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Great, I saw you were fixing the fence earlier, it looks like you've got the hang of this now! Let me give you something for your effort!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Well, I tend the hardwood grove nearby, you can enter if you like and harvest some of the very fine hardwood that grows within.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Apart from that I'm a simple Tai Bwo Wannai villager...I like to try and find out what's going on in the mainland and the rest of the known world.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Well Bwana, if you're stuck for something to do, you could always do a bit of handymanship about the village and cut away the jungle that lies here about.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: If you's is good with da machete Bwana, you's can get's some thatching spars and put them to use repairing the run-down village fence.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Apart from cutting back the jungle and repairing the fence, I think we've got most things covered now Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do I get anything for doing this?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Yes Bwana, you gain experience in woodcutting. In addition many members of the community will reward you with trading sticks, our local currency, for your efforts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trading sticks?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Correct Bwana, trading sticks! It's our form of local currency! Everything you do to help out in the village will give you some favour, but there's a maximum limit!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: When you've reached one hundred percent, you cannot gain any more favour, you need to cash in your favour for trading sticks before you can increase your favour again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who do I get trading sticks from?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Well Bwana, there's a good ten villagers I know of who would show their appreciation for your effort. Who do you want to ask about?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Sharimika is married to Mamma Bufetta, Bwana. They live in the houses just in front of the tribal statue.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Mamma Bufetta lives with her family in the building that faces the stone statue, she lives there with her husband Sharimika and her daughter Layleen.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Layleen is a young girl, the daughter of Mamma Bufetta and Sharimika, she lives with her parents.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Karaday is a young man and his chosen profession is fishing, he follows after his father Safta Doc in that respect. You can find him usually around the center of the village, that is when he's not off fishing.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Safta Doc used to be a fisherman, but he's just about retired now. He has an interesting profession which you might like to talk to him about. You'll find him usually near the building with the anvil in it.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: It's just slightly north of the village statue.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Gabooty is a bit of a mystery, no one's sure where he came from! He's quite helpful though, you can find him in the middle of the village enclosure, to the south of Sharimika's house.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Fanellaman was a fisherman, he's retired now, but he'll try and regale you with tales of his salty sea dog days. He's often walking up and down the western beach.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Jagbakoba is a wild hunter. He can be a bit aggressive but he'll reward anyone who helps to keep the village in good order. You'll find him to the south of the enclosure, near the fires.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Ah Rionasta! He's an enterprising man! Had a very good idea and is now much in demand, you'll find him between the blacksmith's and Timfraku's hut.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Murcaily! That's me! Well, what do you want to know?! I look after the teak and mahogany trees and make sure that we get payment of anyone who uses the facility. And I'm usually standing just about here!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Hey, I've seen you working around the village. You've been doing a lot of good work around here for us. Let me give you something for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a machete from?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: You can purchase a standard machete from Jiminua's store. Gabooty has a selection of local items which you may like to purchase, including better machetes. Also, you may like to chat to Safta Doc, he may have a deal you'll find interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is this jungle?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: You'll find much of it to the southern end of Tai Bwo Wannai, outside of the village. Some is tougher than others so you may have difficulties cutting it back.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Beware though Bwana, those bushes can conceal a great many dangers or trophies, it could be the death... or the making of you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by that?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: It means that you may find something you wish you hadn't but then discover something you never thought you could! If you find me too vague, go and have a chat with Sharimika about it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the fence that needs to be repaired?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: This is to the South Bwana, you should see that there are sections which are rotten, but some other passers-by sometimes help out repairing it.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: You'll need some thatching spars and a machete in order to repair the fence though Bwana.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been doing some work around the village.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Hey, I've seen you working around the village. You've been doing a lot of good work around here for us. Let me give you something for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: I've already given you some trading sticks and I don't have any more on me at the moment. Why not go and talk to some of the other villagers and get some contributions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks.

sections.title: Opening Hardwood Grove Doors with elite Karamja diary

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Hey there, you wanna go in there to chop da special wood, for you my friend, it's free!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nah thanks, I'll give it a miss at the moment I think.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Fair enough, pop back if you change your mind.

sections.title: If the player is wearing a tribal mask

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh - Get away from me you beast!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the problem....oh, I'm wearing this big scary mask! I'd better take it off before I start talking to the natives.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Waahhhhh! - It's da evil broodoo beast come ta eat my very soul!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh - It's a monster!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhh!!
